user_query:
I’m preparing a book review for a local history journal and two related messages. Please critique and rewrite the attached drafts, and produce the outputs below under the outputs/ directory.

Inputs (already in workspace):
- input/review_draft.md
- input/cover_letter.txt
- input/reading_group_invite.txt
- input/bibliography_notes.csv (lookup table for citation placeholders in the review)

What to deliver:
1) Rewrite the book review to outputs/review_rewrite.md with these constraints:
   - 400–450 words, British English spelling.
   - Keep all factual details present in the draft (e.g., dates, edition description) and mention “Isle of Man” at least once.
   - Preserve any quoted phrases exactly as they appear in the draft (do not alter text within quotation marks).
   - Replace each citation placeholder [CITE:KEY] with the matching short_citation from input/bibliography_notes.csv. The draft includes two placeholders; resolve both and leave no placeholders in the final text.
   - Adjust tone to fair, professional, and constructive. Remove pejorative or ad hominem phrasing while still giving specific, evidence-based critique.

2) Rewrite the cover email to outputs/cover_letter_rewrite.txt with these constraints:
   - Include a Subject line at the top (e.g., “Subject: Book review submission — Manx Rural Customs”), followed by the email body.
   - Body length 120–150 words (do not count the Subject line in the word count).
   - Use “Dear Editor,” as the greeting and keep the sender’s name and contact details from the original.
   - Improve clarity and brevity; professional and courteous tone.

3) Rewrite the reading group invitation to outputs/reading_group_invite_rewrite.txt with these constraints:
   - 90–110 words.
   - Retain the exact date, time, and place string as it appears in the draft: “Thursday 23 May, 18:30, Douglas Library Reading Room”.
   - Keep the RSVP email address from the original.
   - Make the tone warm but concise; remove rambling and repetition.

4) Write a structured critique report to outputs/critique_report.json capturing your judgments and checks, as an array with one object per source file. For each object include:
   - file_name (string)
   - original_word_count (integer)
   - revised_word_count (integer)
   - length_within_spec (boolean)
   - tone_summary_before (3–7 words)
   - tone_summary_after (3–7 words)
   - issues_detected (array of short strings, e.g., “pejorative language: ‘frankly tedious’”, “greeting too informal”)
   - For the review object only: citations_resolved (object mapping placeholder keys to the exact short_citation strings used), and quotes_preserved (boolean indicating all quoted phrases in the draft appear verbatim in the rewrite).
   - For the cover letter object only: subject_added (boolean) and greeting_fixed (boolean).
   - For the invite object only: details_preserved (boolean indicating the exact date/time/place string is unchanged in the rewrite) and rsvp_preserved (boolean indicating the same email address is present).

Please ensure all three rewrites and the JSON report are created, and that the CSV mapping is used to resolve all review citations. Do not add external sources. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
